## ERROR BARS chart, ABRID style

Render as SVG (or equivalent) on a 960x540 px canvas.
Every position, size, and color below is exact. No adjectives, no improvisation.

### AI metadata (for agent routing)
```json
{
  "code": "CH-DST-10",
  "ovp_version": "1.0",
  "intent": [
    "uncertainty",
    "comparison",
    "variance"
  ],
  "form": "dot with low-high interval per category",
  "data_required": "categories with a central value and a low-high range (spread, CI, min-max)",
  "family": "DST",
  "alternatives": [
    "CH-DST-04 (full distribution shape, planned)"
  ],
  "see_instead": [
    {
      "when": "only the central value matters",
      "use": "CH-RNK-01"
    }
  ]
}
```

Use when:
- estimates that carry a range: durations, forecasts, measurement spread
- comparing both level AND uncertainty across categories

Do NOT use when:
- ranges are negligible (use CH-RNK-01)
- full distribution shape matters (boxplot when built)

### Input data shape
```json
{
  "title": "action title stating value and spread",
  "eyebrow": "caps, DL chrome only",
  "source": "bottom-left always; states what the range represents",
  "unit": "on the first label",
  "categories": "array of {label, value, low, high, highlight?}, low <= value <= high"
}
```

### Rules
- the interval is a capped line; the dot is the estimate
- labels state value AND range
- one highlight maximum
- what the range means (CI, min-max, spread) is stated in title or source

### Never do this
- error bars without saying what they represent
- bars with whiskers (length + interval double-encodes)
- hiding uncertainty to look confident

### QA before delivering
- [ ] low <= value <= high on every row
- [ ] range semantics stated
- [ ] highlight <= 1
- [ ] unit once
- [ ] source present

### Colors (resolved, use these exact values)

| Element | Color |
|---|---|
| background | `#FFFFFF` |
| interval | `#0E3A2C` |
| dot | `#A7C4B5` |
| dot_highlight | `#197A56` |
| category_label | `#3E4C46` |
| value_label | `#0E3A2C` |
| baseline | `#C9D2CC` |
| title | `#0E3A2C` |
| source | `#3E4C46` |

### Fonts

- Title font: 'Segoe UI',Arial,sans-serif, weight 700
- All other text: 'Segoe UI',Arial,sans-serif, weight 400 (600 on highlighted items)

### Language chrome

No kicker, no eyebrow; title at the default y.
